title: From ad tech tax to AI data brokers: the new middlemen keep 100%, publishers say
description: For some publishers, third-party content scraping lands as an even bigger affront than the ad tech tax they’ve spent years navigating – not a share of the pie, but the pie itself.

title: Winx Health has tripled its retail revenue in the last three months. Now, it’s launching into Walmart
description: Winx Health, a women's health startup, is continuing its push into more U.S. doors through a partnership with Walmart.

title: Legacy retailers like Party City and Staples are increasingly teaming up on shop-in-shops in a bid for relevance
description: Retailers like Staples and Bed Bath & Beyond are identifying opportunities to scoop up struggling brands — like Party City and The Container Store — and using their name recognition to bring new customers into stores.

title: Why Aldi, Walmart and more are redesigning their private-label packaging
description: Major retailers like Target, Walmart and Aldi have recently announced redesigns of their private-label brands. The recent string of rebrands signals new investment in how these products are perceived.

title: Get Ready for Connections 2026: Top Sessions and New Reveals
description: Discover how agentic AI, new Agentforce Marketing features and hands-on skill building will transform your marketing strategy this June in Chicago.

title: Agentic Marketing Platform for Enterprises Valued at $2.75B
description: Native AI vendors are popping up in the marketing world.

title: Media Buying Briefing: Rival trade bodies emerge to contend programmatic’s future
description: Agentic media planning and buying could shake up programmatic media. But old politics and new ambitions are dividing key players into camps pursuing consensus – or disruption.

title: The state of generative AI in the creator economy
description: A look at how the creator economy is using generative AI, from workflow help to identifying partnerships in DMs.

title: ‘This is fine’ creator says AI startup stole his art
description: The ad comes from Artisan, the AI startup behind billboards urging businesses to "stop hiring humans."

title: In Harvard study, AI offered more accurate emergency room diagnoses than two human doctors
description: A new study examines how large language models perform in a variety of medical contexts, including real emergency room cases — where at least one model seemed to be more accurate than human doctors.

title: AI-generated actors and scripts are now ineligible for Oscars
description: Bad news for Tilly Norwood.

title: The best AI dictation apps, tested and ranked
description: AI-powered dictation apps are useful for replying to emails, taking notes, and even coding through your voice

title: Musk v. Altman week 1: Elon Musk says he was duped, warns AI could kill us all, and admits that xAI distills OpenAI’s models
description: In the first week of the landmark trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI, Musk took the stand in a crisp black suit and tie and argued that OpenAI CEO Sam Altman and president Greg Brockman had deceived him into bankrolling the company. Along the way, he warned that AI could destroy us all and sat through…

title: Cyber-Insecurity in the AI Era
description: Cybersecurity was already under strain before AI entered the stack. Now, as AI expands the attack surface and adds new complexity, the limits of legacy approaches are becoming harder to ignore. This session from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference explores why security must be rethought with AI at its core, not layered on after…

title: Operationalizing AI for Scale and Sovereignty
description: Companies are taking control of their own data to tailor AI for their needs. The challenge lies in balancing ownership with the safe, trusted flow of high‑quality data needed to power reliable insights. This conversation from MIT Technology Review’s EmTech AI conference examines how AI factories unlock new levels of scale, sustainability, and governance—positioning data…

title: Sell Locally and Now Into Regional Markets: Temu’s Local Seller Program Explained (2026 Update)
description:
Sponsored content is created on behalf of Digital Commerce 360. Our editorial staff is not involved in the creation of the sponsored content. Temu is a global e-commerce platform that helps businesses reach more customers and grow online. As part of that effort, Temu launched its Local Seller Program to enable eligible local businesses to […]

title: New Ecommerce Tools: April 28, 2026
description:
This week: AI-powered advertising, video ad generators, B2B commerce, fraud prevention, box-free returns, loyalty programs, agentic commerce, contact centers, and more.
The post New Ecommerce Tools: April 28, 2026 appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: Chrome Extensions for GenAI Visibility
description:
Testing and experimenting reveal priorities for elevating visibility in genAI platforms. 
The post Chrome Extensions for GenAI Visibility appeared first on Practical Ecommerce.

title: GameStop’s $56B eBay bid aims to create collectibles powerhouse
description: The gaming retailer submitted an unsolicited acquisition proposal for the online marketplace and suggested its store fleet could serve as eBay's fulfillment network.

title: Saks Global expects to exit bankruptcy in June with $1.2B in debt
description: The luxury retail conglomerate said it would be profitable in three years and that, starting next fiscal year, revenue growth would average 7% annually.

title: Target launches new kind of supply chain facility
description: The Houston Receive Center gives the retailer more inventory-holding capacity before sending products to downstream locations.

title: GameStop proposes acquisition of eBay without clarifying funding
description:
GameStop has submitted a proposal to acquire all of the online marketplace eBay, which made about as much in sales during its most recent fiscal quarter as the retail chain did in its most recent full fiscal year. The proposal would have GameStop acquire eBay for about $55.5 billion, following customary closing conditions. It is […]
The post GameStop proposes acquisition of eBay without clarifying funding appeared first on Digital Commerce 360.

title: Our Vision for Building an Open Ecosystem for the Agent Era
description: For years, HubSpot invested in making our platform the best place for marketing, sales, and service teams to do their work. With AI, we’ve been building it to do the work for them – through agents that qualify leads, resolve tickets, save deals, and drive outcomes across the business. That’s why we call HubSpot an agentic customer platform.

title: DoorDash adds AI tools to speed up merchant onboarding, edit photos of dishes
description: DoorDash on Monday added new AI-powered tools that let merchants speed up onboarding, edit photos to make dishes look better, and create new websites from existing content.

title: Dollar General bridges onsite, offsite retail media with new solution
description: Brands will be able to activate and optimize offsite and onsite inventory within The Trade Desk for the first time through an integration supported by Kevel.

title: Inside Opal’s attempt to reduce AI’s ‘alignment tax’ for marketers
description: The planning platform's new AI copilot, Gem, is designed to align campaign data with overall strategy and help teams understand results faster.

title: DoorDash delivers memes, reality TV moms for Mother’s Day campaign
description: A hero spot and social content spotlight the mom group chat as part of an effort that includes deals with Ulta, Old Navy and more.

title: Omnicom credits Q1 wins to core operations, reaffirms ongoing cost cuts
description: The company also touted the success of its AI-powered Omni marketing platform, which has now been scaled across the entire organization.

title: How Qualcomm is building an iconic consumer brand for the AI era
description: CMO Don McGuire details the tech giant’s work to develop consumer brand Snapdragon, which culminates in a new campaign by 72andSunny.

title: How Marketers Can Get Clean Incrementality Measurements in a Messy World
description: This post was created in partnership with Fetch Key takeaways Marketers have more data than ever, yet many still struggle to answer a basic question: Did a campaign change behavior […]

title: How marketing leaders are succeeding in the AI era
description:
Despite AI reshaping markets, most CMOs are still seen as execution-focused, creating a gap that threatens their strategic relevance.
The post How marketing leaders are succeeding in the AI era appeared first on MarTech.

title: Why AI agents need decision authority
description:
Data sovereignty controls access, but decision architecture defines action. Beyond unifying data, a sovereign operating layer secures AI rights and prevents risk.
The post Why AI agents need decision authority appeared first on MarTech.

title: Why SaaS is moving from features to outcomes
description:
AI is compressing feature value and shifting how SaaS gets priced. Outcomes now drive growth, differentiation, and monetization.
The post Why SaaS is moving from features to outcomes appeared first on MarTech.

title: Why brand authority beats topical authority in AI search
description: More content won’t make you an authority. AI search rewards brands with visibility, mentions, and real demand.
